_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:08:44Z)**_

`specstory login` requires interactive browser authentication — it can't run in a non-interactive terminal. You'll need to run it directly in your terminal:

```bash
specstory login
```

It will open `https://cloud.specstory.com/cli-login` in your browser, then prompt you to enter the 6-character code shown there.

Once authenticated, run either sync option from your project directory:

```bash
# Sync all existing conversations
specstory sync

# Or run Claude Code with auto-sync going forward
specstory run claude
```

— Superman, the CLI itself is installed and ready. Just run `specstory login` interactively in your terminal to complete auth.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:10:39Z)**_

Open a new terminal window (Terminal.app or iTerm2), then run:

```bash
specstory login
```

It will print:

```
🔑 Enter the 6-character code shown in your browser (or 'quit' to cancel):
   Code:
```

Type `xGp-fqU` at that `Code:` prompt and press **Enter**.

The key point: this must happen in a separate terminal window, not inside Claude Code's chat interface — the CLI needs interactive stdin which Claude Code's tool runner doesn't provide.

```text
  SpecStory is a wrapper for terminal coding agents that auto-saves markdown files of all your chat interactions.       
                                                                                                                        
  Supported agents: claude (Claude Code), codex (Codex CLI), cursor (Cursor CLI), droid (Factory Droid CLI), gemini     
  (Gemini CLI).                                                                                                         
         
  USAGE  
         
    specstory [command] [--flags]                                                           
            
  EXAMPLES  
            
    # Check terminal coding agents installation                                             
    specstory check                                                                         
                                                                                            
    # Run the default agent with auto-saving                                                
    specstory run                                                                           
                                                                                            
    # Run a specific agent with auto-saving                                                 
    specstory run claude                                                                    
    specstory run codex                                                                     
    specstory run cursor                                                                    
    specstory run droid                                                                     
    specstory run gemini                                                                    
                                                                                            
    # Run with custom command                                                               
    specstory run claude -c "/custom/path/to/agent"                                         
                                                                                            
    # Generate markdown files for all agent sessions associated with the current directory  
    specstory sync                                                                          
                                                                                            
    # Generate markdown files for specific agent sessions                                   
    specstory sync -s <session-id>                                                          
    specstory sync -s <session-id-1> -s <session-id-2>                                      
                                                                                            
    # Watch for any agent activity in the current directory and generate markdown files     
    specstory watch                                                                         
            
  COMMANDS  
            
    check [provider-id] [--flags]  Check if the configuration is valid and terminal coding agents are properly installed
    help [command]                 Help about any command
    list [provider-id] [--flags]   List all sessions for terminal coding agents
    login [--flags]                Log in to SpecStory Cloud
    logout [--flags]               Log out from SpecStory Cloud
    run [provider-id] [--flags]    Launch terminal coding agents in interactive mode with auto-save
    sync [provider-id] [--flags]   Sync markdown files for terminal coding agent sessions
    version                        Show SpecStory version information
    watch [provider-id] [--flags]  Watch for coding agent activity with auto-save
         
  FLAGS  
         
    --console                      Enable error/warn/info output to stdout
    --debug                        Enable debug-level output (requires --console or --log)
    -h --help                      Help for specstory
    --log                          Write error/warn/info output to ./.specstory/debug/debug.log
    --no-usage-analytics           Disable usage analytics
    --no-version-check             Skip checking for newer versions
    --silent                       Suppress all non-error output
    -v --version                   Version for specstory

---

  Create or update markdown files for the agent sessions in the current working directory.                              
                                                                                                                        
  By default, syncs all registered providers that have activity.                                                        
  Provide a specific agent ID to sync a specific provider.                                                              
                                                                                                                        
  Available provider IDs: claude (Claude Code), codex (Codex CLI), cursor (Cursor CLI), droid (Factory Droid CLI),      
  gemini (Gemini CLI).                                                                                                  
         
  USAGE  
         
    specstory sync [provider-id] [--flags]                                
            
  EXAMPLES  
            
    # Sync all agents with activity                                       
    specstory sync                                                        
                                                                          
    # Sync specific agent                                                 
    specstory sync claude                                                 
    specstory sync codex                                                  
    specstory sync cursor                                                 
    specstory sync droid                                                  
    specstory sync gemini                                                 
                                                                          
    # Sync a specific session by UUID                                     
    specstory sync -s <session-id>                                        
                                                                          
    # Sync multiple sessions                                              
    specstory sync -s <session-id-1> -s <session-id-2> -s <session-id-3>  
                                                                          
    # Output session markdown to stdout without saving                    
    specstory sync -s <session-id> --print                                
                                                                          
    # Output multiple sessions to stdout                                  
    specstory sync -s <session-id-1> -s <session-id-2> --print            
                                                                          
    # Sync all sessions for the current directory, with console output    
    specstory sync --console                                              
                                                                          
    # Sync all sessions for the current directory, with a log file        
    specstory sync --log                                                  
         
  FLAGS  
         
    --console                 Enable error/warn/info output to stdout
    --debug                   Enable debug-level output (requires --console or --log)
    --debug-dir               Custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)
    -h --help                 Help for sync
    --local-time-zone         Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)
    --log                     Write error/warn/info output to ./.specstory/debug/debug.log
    --no-cloud-sync           Disable cloud sync functionality
    --no-telemetry-prompts    Exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled
    --no-usage-analytics      Disable usage analytics
    --no-version-check        Skip checking for newer versions
    --only-cloud-sync         Skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)
    --only-stats              Only update statistics, skip local markdown files and cloud sync
    --output-dir              Custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)
    --print                   Output session markdown to stdout instead of saving (requires -s flag)
    -s --session              Optional session IDs to sync (can be specified multiple times, provider-specific format)
    --silent                  Suppress all non-error output
    --telemetry-endpoint      Open Telemetry Protocol (OTLP) gRPC collector endpoint (default is off, e.g., localhost:4317)
    --telemetry-service-name  Override the default service name for telemetry, if telemetry is enabled
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:11:48Z)**_

Two options:

**Option 1 — Auto-sync going forward (recommended)**

Instead of running `claude` directly, launch Claude Code through SpecStory in your terminal:

```bash
cd /Users/shivpat/harmony-forge-redesign
specstory run claude
```

Every conversation is automatically saved as markdown to `.specstory/history/` as you chat.

**Option 2 — Sync past conversations now**

From your project directory:

```bash
cd /Users/shivpat/harmony-forge-redesign
specstory sync claude --console
```

This converts all existing Claude Code session history for this project into markdown files in `.specstory/history/`.

**Option 3 — Watch mode (background)**

```bash
specstory watch claude
```

Watches for any Claude Code activity and saves automatically without wrapping the CLI.

The markdown files end up at `.specstory/history/` relative to whichever directory you run the command from. — Superman

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:14:07Z)**_

Everything is set. Here's what was done:

**Past conversations** — synced to [.specstory/history/](.specstory/history/) (108 JSONL session files converted to dated markdown).

**Autonomous saving** — a macOS launchd service (`com.specstory.watch`) is now running and will:
- Auto-start on every login
- Watch for any Claude Code activity across your machine
- Save conversations to `.specstory/history/` automatically

**Logs** if you ever need to debug:
- `~/.specstory/watch.log`
- `~/.specstory/watch-error.log`

To stop/start manually:
```bash
launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.specstory.watch.plist
launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.specstory.watch.plist
```

— Superman, you're fully set up. Every future Claude Code session will be auto-archived.

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — HarmonyForge (Root)
     2→
     3→*Project-wide context. Every agent operating anywhere in this repository inherits these terms, decisions, patterns, and policies.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **HarmonyForge** | An ante-hoc "Glass Box" co-creative system for SATB music arrangement |
    12→| **Tactile Sandbox** | The primary editing screen — symbolic score rendering (VexFlow) + playback (Tone.js) |
    13→| **Theory Inspector** | The sidebar UI — LLM-driven Glass Box reasoning and Red Line violation flags |
    14→| **Glass Box** | The design principle: all reasoning is visible and anchored in academic sources, never opaque |
    15→| **Intrinsic Determinism** | Every claim is anchored to an academic definition, not probabilistic folk knowledge |
    16→| **Red Line** | Inline violation annotations on the VexFlow canvas (parallel fifths, voice crossings, etc.) |
    17→| **SATB** | Soprano, Alto, Tenor, Bass — the four-voice arrangement format this system specializes in |
    18→| **Symbolic** | Music represented as pitch/duration/voice data (MusicXML/JSON) — never raw audio or waveforms |
    19→| **Nocturne** | The dark color palette / theme for HarmonyForge |
    20→| **Sonata** | The light color palette / theme for HarmonyForge |
    21→| **Superman** | The human owner of this project; the final authority on all decisions |
    22→| **Ralph Wiggum Loop** | The autonomous execution pattern: loop.sh → PROMPT_build.md → one task → backpressure → commit |
    23→| **Backpressure** | `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` must pass before every commit; failure is a hard stop |
    24→| **FIC** | Frequent Intentional Compaction — the upstream planning workflow (research → plan → implement) |
    25→| **ADR** | Architecture Decision Record — permanent, immutable log of why a technical decision was made |
    26→| **Spec** | JTBD specification document — observable user outcomes with testable acceptance criteria |
    27→| **JTBD** | Job to Be Done — the user outcome framing used inside every spec |
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## Decisions
    32→
    33→| ADR | Decision | Status |
    34→|---|---|---|
    35→| [ADR-001](docs/adr/ADR-001-adopt-ralph-wiggum-loop.md) | Adopt Ralph Wiggum Loop as primary autonomous execution pattern | Accepted |
    36→
    37→All ADRs live in `docs/adr/`. Index: `docs/adr/overview.md`.
    38→
    39→---
    40→
    41→## Patterns
    42→
    43→- **Three Gears**: Specification (Gear 1) → Planning with planner (Gear 2) → Execution via loop (Gear 3)
    44→- **HCI Protocol**: Every interactive session follows preview → gate → execute (`.claude/rules/hci-protocol.md`)
    45→- **Theory Named Strategy**: Every music theory claim is anchored — "X is defined as [definition], per [source]"
    46→- **Architecture Boundary**: Frontend renders/schedules/surfaces; constraint logic lives in the backend only
    47→- **Atomic Design**: UI components are organized as atoms → molecules → organisms
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## Policies

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:32:26Z)**_

`CONTEXT.md` is the **project-wide knowledge header** for HarmonyForge — a single document every AI agent inherits when operating in this repo. It has four sections:

| Section | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Ontology** | Defines the project's vocabulary — terms like "Glass Box", "Red Line", "Nocturne", "Ralph Wiggum Loop", so all agents use the same language |
| **Decisions** | Index of ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) — permanent logs of *why* technical choices were made |
| **Patterns** | The key recurring workflows: Three Gears, HCI Protocol, Theory Named Strategy, Architecture Boundary |
| **Policies** | Hard rules: never run `loop.sh` on main, never use ad-hoc hex colors, never put constraint logic in the frontend |

**Why it exists:** When an agent (Claude, Gemini, etc.) starts a task, it reads CONTEXT.md first. This ensures every agent — across sessions and tools — shares the same mental model of the project without needing to re-derive it from the codebase each time. It's the "minimum viable shared context."

There are also scoped `CONTEXT.md` files in subdirectories (e.g. `src/app/CONTEXT.md`, `docs/CONTEXT.md`) that extend this root with directory-specific context.

— Superman

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/
     2→
     3→*Frontend source root. All React components, hooks, stores, utilities, and styles live here. This directory is the entire frontend boundary.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Frontend Boundary** | The explicit line: frontend renders, schedules, and surfaces. It never computes music theory. |
    12→| **Component-Driven** | UI is composed of atomic, reusable TypeScript components — no monolithic page files |
    13→| **Strict TypeScript** | `tsconfig.json` has `"strict": true` — no `any`, no implicit `undefined`, explicit interfaces everywhere |
    14→| **Tailwind CSS** | Utility-class-only styling — no CSS-in-JS, no inline styles, no ad-hoc color values |
    15→| **"use client"** | Next.js directive placed at the component level, not the page level, for granular hydration control |
    16→| **Token** | A named CSS custom property from `globals.css` representing a design system value (color, spacing, etc.) |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **App Router (not Pages Router)**: All routes use Next.js App Router conventions (`layout.tsx`, `page.tsx`, route segments)
    23→- **Zustand over Context API**: Selected for performance (selective subscription) and TypeScript ergonomics
    24→- **Tailwind v4 CSS-first**: No `tailwind.config.js` — tokens are declared in `src/app/globals.css` as CSS custom properties
    25→- **Atomic Design**: Components organized as `atoms/` → `molecules/` → `organisms/` with no logic leaking downward
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Patterns
    30→
    31→- All components: `React.forwardRef` for interactive elements; `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for class merging
    32→- All props: explicit TypeScript interfaces — never `React.FC<any>` or prop spreading without a type
    33→- All state: reads from Zustand via `useStore(selector)` — components are stateless except for mounting guards
    34→- All VexFlow DOM manipulation: inside `useEffect` only, never in render
    35→- All Tone.js scheduling: symbolic events only via `usePlayback` hook — no `AudioContext` or raw buffer access
    36→- Accessibility: `aria-label`, `aria-pressed`, `role`, and `focus-visible:` classes required on all interactive elements
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Policies
    41→
    42→- **No constraint logic** — no parallel fifth detection, voice crossing detection, or SATB validation in any `src/` file
    43→- **No ad-hoc hex values** — every color reference must resolve to a `--nocturne-*` or `--sonata-*` token
    44→- **No `any` types** — define explicit interfaces; use `unknown` + type narrowing if the shape is uncertain
    45→- **No direct DOM manipulation** outside of `useEffect` in VexFlow components
    46→- **No raw audio** — `AudioContext`, `createOscillator`, `decodeAudioData`, and similar Web Audio API calls are banned
    47→- **No API calls inside Zustand stores** — stores hold state and synchronous actions only
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## Research
    52→
    53→| Resource | Purpose |
    54→|---|---|
    55→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Code style, stack constraints, token rules |
    56→| `src/app/globals.css` | All design tokens (Nocturne/Sonata palettes, spacing, radius, shadows) |
    57→| `src/styles/fonts.css` | Font imports (Instrument Serif, Inter, Satoshi) |
    58→| `src/lib/utils.ts` | `cn()` class merging utility |
    59→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | Acceptance criteria for every component in this directory |
    60→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./src/components/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/
     2→
     3→*Component library root. All UI building blocks live here, organized by Atomic Design: atoms → molecules → organisms.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Atom** | The smallest indivisible UI element — a button, icon, chip, toggle. Stateless beyond mounting. |
    12→| **Molecule** | A compound of atoms serving a single user task — a playback bar, a violation card, a step navigator |
    13→| **Organism** | A full UI section that may own layout and store reads — the score canvas, the Theory Inspector panel, the sandbox header |
    14→| **`cn()`** | The class name merging utility from `src/lib/utils.ts` — always use this for conditional Tailwind classes |
    15→| **`forwardRef`** | React pattern for exposing a ref on interactive elements — required for atoms and molecules that wrap native elements |
    16→| **Display Name** | `ComponentName.displayName = "ComponentName"` — required on every `forwardRef` component for DevTools readability |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **Atomic Design (not feature-based folders)**: Components are organized by complexity, not by screen. This prevents coupling and enables composition.
    23→- **No index barrel exports**: Import directly from the component file path — avoids circular dependency and tree-shaking issues
    24→- **`cn()` over string concatenation**: All conditional class logic goes through `cn()` to preserve Tailwind's PurgeCSS compatibility
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Patterns
    29→
    30→- File naming: `PascalCase.tsx` — one component per file
    31→- Export pattern: named export (not default) — `export const MyComponent = ...`
    32→- Props: explicit interface `interface MyComponentProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>`
    33→- Class merging: `className={cn("base-classes", conditionalClass && "conditional", className)}`
    34→- Interactive elements: always include `aria-label` and `focus-visible:` Tailwind class
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Policies
    39→
    40→- **No business logic in any component** — components render state; they do not compute it
    41→- **No direct Zustand store mutations in atoms** — atoms receive callbacks via props
    42→- **No hardcoded hex values anywhere** — every color must resolve to a Nocturne/Sonata token
    43→- **No VexFlow in atoms or molecules** — VexFlow DOM manipulation belongs in organisms only
    44→- **No API calls** — components are purely presentational; data fetching belongs in hooks or server components
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Research
    49→
    50→| Resource | Purpose |
    51→|---|---|
    52→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Code style, prop typing, token rules |
    53→| `src/lib/utils.ts` | `cn()` implementation |
    54→| `src/app/globals.css` | All available design tokens |
    55→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | Acceptance criteria that map to specific components |
    56→
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/app/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/app/
     2→
     3→*Next.js App Router root. Contains the global layout, global CSS tokens, and the two primary route segments: `sandbox/` and `document/`.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Route Segment** | A Next.js App Router directory with a `page.tsx` that maps to a URL path |
    12→| **Root Layout** | `layout.tsx` — wraps all routes; mounts `ThemeProvider`, global fonts, and `globals.css` |
    13→| **Root Page** | `page.tsx` — the landing screen (`/`); currently the entry point |
    14→| **Sandbox Route** | `/sandbox` — the Tactile Sandbox: score canvas, playback, Theory Inspector |
    15→| **Document Route** | `/document` — upload, metadata, export |
    16→| **globals.css** | The single source of truth for all design tokens (Tailwind v4 CSS-first) |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **App Router (not Pages Router)**: Layouts, loading states, and error boundaries are file-based
    23→- **`globals.css` in `src/app/`**: Tailwind v4 CSS-first — tokens declared as CSS custom properties inside `@theme {}` and `:root {}` — no `tailwind.config.js`
    24→- **`"use client"` at component level**: Pages and layouts remain Server Components by default; `"use client"` is pushed down to individual components that need interactivity
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Patterns
    29→
    30→- `layout.tsx` imports `globals.css`, `fonts.css`, and mounts `<ThemeProvider>` — nothing else
    31→- `page.tsx` files compose organisms only — no inline logic, no direct store reads
    32→- Token structure in `globals.css`: `@theme {}` for Tailwind mapping → `:root {}` for CSS variables → `.dark {}` for Nocturne overrides
    33→- Font loading: `next/font` or `@import` in `src/styles/fonts.css` — never `<link>` in layout
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Policies
    38→
    39→- **No business logic in `layout.tsx` or root `page.tsx`** — layout is structural only
    40→- **No constraint computation in any route** — the route layer composes UI; it does not process music theory
    41→- **Do not add design tokens anywhere except `globals.css`** — all token additions go here, nowhere else
    42→- **Never nest a route segment inside another segment** unless creating a true sub-route (e.g., `/sandbox/[id]`)
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Research
    47→
    48→| Resource | Purpose |
    49→|---|---|
    50→| `src/app/globals.css` | All design tokens — Sonata/Nocturne, spacing, radius, shadows, WCAG-safe accent |
    51→| `src/styles/fonts.css` | Font face imports |
    52→| `src/components/atoms/ThemeProvider.tsx` | `next-themes` provider mounted in layout |
    53→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-004 (theme toggle), AC-001 (score canvas route) |
    54→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/app/sandbox/
     2→
     3→*The Tactile Sandbox route (`/sandbox`). This is the primary user-facing screen: VexFlow score canvas, Tone.js symbolic playback, Theory Inspector sidebar, and inline Red Line violation tooltips.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Tactile Sandbox** | The full editing experience — compose, play back, and inspect a symbolic SATB arrangement |
    12→| **Score Canvas** | The VexFlow-rendered area displaying all four SATB voices as a readable score |
    13→| **Playhead** | The position marker that advances across the score during playback |
    14→| **Theory Inspector** | The collapsible sidebar that displays Glass Box reasoning and Red Line violation details |
    15→| **Red Line** | An inline visual annotation on the score canvas marking a voice-leading violation |
    16→| **Context Menu** | The right-click menu on the score canvas (managed by `useSandboxStore`) |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **Sandbox is its own route segment** — isolated from `/document` so score editing state does not persist across navigation
    23→- **Organisms compose the page** — `page.tsx` assembles `SandboxHeader`, `ScoreCanvas`, `TheoryInspectorPanel`; no inline UI logic
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Patterns
    28→
    29→- `page.tsx` imports and composes organisms only:
    30→  ```tsx
    31→  import { SandboxHeader } from "@/components/organisms/SandboxHeader";
    32→  import { ScoreCanvas } from "@/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas";
    33→  import { TheoryInspectorPanel } from "@/components/organisms/TheoryInspectorPanel";
    34→  ```
    35→- Store reads happen inside organism components, not in `page.tsx`
    36→- Right-click context menu is managed via `useSandboxStore.openContextMenu` / `closeContextMenu`
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Policies
    41→
    42→- **No VexFlow or Tone.js imports in `page.tsx`** — those live in `ScoreCanvas` and `usePlayback`
    43→- **No violation detection in this route** — violations arrive as backend JSON; this route only renders them
    44→- **No MusicXML parsing in this route** — parsing happens in `src/lib/music/`
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Research
    49→
    50→| Resource | Purpose |
    51→|---|---|
    52→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | All ACs for this route (AC-001 through AC-008) |
    53→| `src/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas.tsx` | Primary VexFlow rendering component (363 lines, currently static) |
    54→| `src/components/organisms/TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx` | Theory Inspector sidebar |
    55→| `src/components/organisms/SandboxHeader.tsx` | Header with theme toggle, export, maximize |
    56→| `src/store/useSandboxStore.ts` | Context menu state; needs `isPlaying` + `playheadPosition` (AC-002) |
    57→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/app/document/
     2→
     3→*The Document route (`/document`). Handles MusicXML upload, metadata editing, and export. No score editing or playback happens here.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Document Route** | The upload and export screen — entry point for loading a piece into HarmonyForge |
    12→| **Dropzone** | The drag-and-drop upload area for MusicXML files |
    13→| **Export** | The action of saving the arrangement to MusicXML/PDF format |
    14→| **Metadata** | Piece title, composer, key signature, time signature — displayed and editable here |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **Separate route from sandbox** — document management (upload, export) is a distinct user task from editing; no shared layout state
    21→- **Upload handled here, not in sandbox** — the sandwich model: document route is ingress/egress; sandbox is the editing core
    22→
    23→---
    24→

    26→
    27→- `page.tsx` composes: `ScoreDropzone` (upload), `DocumentHeader` (metadata), `ExportModal` (export)
    28→- File upload result (parsed MusicXML JSON) is dispatched to a Zustand store slice before navigating to `/sandbox`
    29→
    30→---
    31→

    33→
    34→- **No score rendering in this route** — VexFlow belongs in `/sandbox` only
    35→- **No Tone.js in this route** — playback is a sandbox-only feature
    36→- **No constraint computation** — MusicXML parsing is a data transformation; violation detection is backend-only

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/components/atoms/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/atoms/
     2→
     3→*Primitive, indivisible UI elements. Each atom is stateless beyond a mounting guard. No atoms write to Zustand or call APIs.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Atom** | The smallest reusable UI unit — wraps a single HTML element or SVG with HarmonyForge styling applied |
    12→| **Mounting Guard** | A `React.useState(false)` + `useEffect(() => setMounted(true), [])` pattern used to prevent SSR hydration mismatches (see `ThemeToggle`) |
    13→| **Token Reference** | A Tailwind class that resolves to a CSS custom property (e.g., `bg-[--hf-accent-accessible]`) |
    14→| **SMUFL** | Standard Music Font Layout — the glyph standard used by `SmuflIcon` for musical symbols |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **`ThemeToggle` uses `next-themes` `useTheme()`** — theme state lives in the `next-themes` provider, not Zustand
    21→- **AC-004 constraint violation on `ThemeToggle`**: 6 hardcoded hex values (`#2D1817`, `#A55B3766`, `#C8B8B6`, `#FFB300`, `#1A1110`, `#4A3B39`) must be replaced with design system tokens — requires Superman approval before any fix
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Patterns
    26→
    27→- `forwardRef` + `displayName` on every interactive atom
    28→- `cn()` for all class composition
    29→- Mounting guard pattern in `ThemeToggle` to prevent CLS:
    30→  ```tsx
    31→  const [mounted, setMounted] = React.useState(false);
    32→  React.useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []);
    33→  if (!mounted) return <div className="w-[72px] h-[32px] rounded-full" />;
    34→  ```
    35→- `aria-label` required on every button atom; `aria-pressed` on toggle buttons
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Policies
    40→
    41→- **No Zustand store reads or writes** — atoms are controlled via props only
    42→- **No hardcoded hex values** — replace with token references (`--nocturne-*`, `--sonata-*`, `--hf-*`)
    43→- **No `useEffect` side effects beyond mounting guards** — atoms do not fetch data or schedule timers
    44→- **No VexFlow or Tone.js imports**
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Files
    49→
    50→| File | Purpose | Spec AC |
    51→|---|---|---|
    52→| `BrandTitle.tsx` | Logotype text atom | — |
    53→| `ChatFAB.tsx` | Floating action button for Theory Inspector | — |
    54→| `ExportFormatCard.tsx` | Card atom for export format selection | — |
    55→| `LogoLockup.tsx` | Logo + brand name lockup | — |
    56→| `PartChip.tsx` | Voice/part label chip | AC-007 |
    57→| `SmuflIcon.tsx` | SMUFL musical glyph renderer | — |
    58→| `ThemeProvider.tsx` | `next-themes` provider wrapper | AC-004 |
    59→| `ThemeToggle.tsx` | Light/dark toggle button — **has token violations** | AC-004 |
    60→| `Tooltip.tsx` | Generic tooltip atom | AC-003 |
    61→| `UploadIcon.tsx` | Upload icon atom | — |
    62→
    63→---
    64→
    65→## Research
    66→
    67→| Resource | Purpose |
    68→|---|---|
    69→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-004 (ThemeToggle token fix), AC-007 (PartChip theory tags) |
    70→| `src/app/globals.css` | All valid token names to replace hardcoded values |
    71→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Token rule, forwardRef pattern |
    72→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./src/components/molecules/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/molecules/
     2→
     3→*Compounds of atoms serving a single user task. Molecules may read from Zustand but do not own store slices. No VexFlow DOM manipulation here.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Molecule** | A focused compound component — one user task, composed from atoms |
    12→| **PlaybackBar** | The transport controls molecule: play/pause, position scrubber |
    13→| **ViolationCard** | Renders a single Red Line violation — label, voices, definition |
    14→| **StepBar** | 3-step workflow navigator: Playground → Document → Sandbox |
    15→| **VoiceDropdown** | Voice/part selector dropdown |
    16→| **QuickReplyChips** | Suggested reply chips for the Theory Inspector chat interface |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **`PlaybackBar` is currently unwired**: the component exists but has no `usePlayback` hook connected to Tone.js (AC-002 open)
    23→- **`ViolationCard` is sidebar-only**: violation data renders in the Theory Inspector panel, not inline on the canvas — inline `RedLineTooltip` is a separate organism-level component (AC-003 open)
    24→- **`StepBar` is top-anchored workflow nav**: it is NOT the floating bottom dock for score pagination (AC-005 open) — the dock is a new component
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Patterns
    29→
    30→- Molecules read Zustand state via selector hooks: `const isPlaying = useSandboxStore(s => s.isPlaying)`
    31→- Molecules dispatch actions via callbacks passed as props or via direct store action calls
    32→- Violation data shape expected from backend: `{ measure: number, beat: number, voices: [string, string], ruleLabel: string, definition: string }`
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Policies
    37→
    38→- **No VexFlow imports** — molecules do not touch the score canvas DOM
    39→- **No Tone.js `AudioContext`** — playback molecules dispatch symbolic events to the scheduler hook only
    40→- **No violation detection** — `ViolationCard` renders backend JSON; it never derives violations from note data
    41→- **No hardcoded hex values** — Nocturne/Sonata tokens only
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## Files
    46→
    47→| File | Purpose | Spec AC |
    48→|---|---|---|
    49→| `PlaybackBar.tsx` | Transport controls — unwired | AC-002 |
    50→| `SandboxPlaybackBar.tsx` | Sandbox-specific playback variant — unwired | AC-002 |
    51→| `ViolationCard.tsx` | Sidebar violation display (147 lines) | AC-003 |
    52→| `StepBar.tsx` | Workflow step navigator (top-anchored) | AC-005 |
    53→| `VoiceDropdown.tsx` | Voice/part selector | — |

    62→| `UploadPromptContent.tsx` | Upload prompt copy | — |

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/components/organisms/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/organisms/
     2→
     3→*Full UI sections. Organisms own layout, may own store reads, and compose molecules and atoms. VexFlow DOM manipulation belongs here and only here.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Organism** | A complete, self-contained UI section — the score canvas, the inspector panel, the header |
    12→| **ScoreCanvas** | The VexFlow rendering container — currently 363 lines of static React/Tailwind (not VexFlow-driven) |
    13→| **TheoryInspectorPanel** | The collapsible sidebar — LLM Glass Box reasoning, Red Line violations, voice part analysis |
    14→| **SandboxHeader** | Top bar — theme toggle, export button, maximize button, piece title |
    15→| **SandboxContextMenu** | Right-click menu on the score canvas — sourced from `useSandboxStore` |
    16→| **EnsembleBuilderPanel** | Panel for selecting and configuring the SATB voice ensemble |
    17→| **ScorePalette** | Toolbar for note input and editing tools |
    18→| **Renderer** | VexFlow's `Renderer` class — creates the SVG/canvas element inside `useEffect` |
    19→| **Stave** | VexFlow's `Stave` class — represents one staff line (one voice) |
    20→| **Voice** | VexFlow's `Voice` class — holds the notes for a single SATB part |
    21→| **Formatter** | VexFlow's `Formatter` class — handles note layout and horizontal spacing |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Decisions
    26→
    27→- **`ScoreCanvas` is currently static**: pixel-coordinate React/Tailwind rendering, not VexFlow-driven. VexFlow migration is the highest-complexity Phase 2 task. Split into 3 iterations: (1) VexFlow Stave rendering, (2) dynamic note rendering, (3) ScoreState integration.
    28→- **VexFlow only in `ScoreCanvas`**: No other organism, molecule, or atom may import VexFlow
    29→- **Theory Inspector receives backend JSON**: The panel never computes violations — it renders structured `{ measure, beat, voices, ruleLabel, definition }` from the API
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Patterns
    34→
    35→- VexFlow setup inside `useEffect` with a stable `ref`:
    36→  ```tsx
    37→  const containerRef = React.useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    38→  React.useEffect(() => {
    39→    if (!containerRef.current) return;
    40→    const renderer = new Renderer(containerRef.current, Renderer.Backends.SVG);
    41→    // ... stave + voice + formatter setup
    42→  }, [scoreState]);
    43→  ```
    44→- `ScoreCanvas` wrapper must have `role="img"` and `aria-label="SATB score: [piece name]"` (AC-001)
    45→- `RedLineTooltip` attaches inline at VexFlow note coordinates — uses `aria-describedby` for keyboard accessibility (AC-003)
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## Policies
    50→
    51→- **VexFlow DOM manipulation inside `useEffect` only** — never in render, never in event handlers
    52→- **No constraint logic** — `ScoreCanvas` renders what Zustand `ScoreState` contains; it never validates notes
    53→- **No hardcoded pixel coordinates in the VexFlow migration** — all positions derived from VexFlow layout engine
    54→- **No Theory Inspector sidebar logic in `ScoreCanvas`** — the two organisms are independent
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→## Files
    59→
    60→| File | Purpose | Spec AC | Status |
    61→|---|---|---|---|
    62→| `ScoreCanvas.tsx` | VexFlow score renderer (363 lines, static) | AC-001 | `[~]` partial |
    63→| `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx` | Theory Inspector sidebar | AC-003 | `[~]` partial |
    64→| `SandboxHeader.tsx` | Sandbox top bar | AC-006 | `[~]` partial |
    65→| `SandboxContextMenu.tsx` | Right-click context menu | — | exists |
    66→| `EnsembleBuilderPanel.tsx` | Voice ensemble configuration | — | exists |
    67→| `ExportModal.tsx` | Export dialog | — | exists |
    68→| `PlaygroundBackground.tsx` | Landing playground background | — | exists |
    69→| `ScoreDropzone.tsx` | MusicXML upload dropzone | — | exists |
    70→| `ScorePalette.tsx` | Note input toolbar | — | exists |
    71→| `ScorePreviewPanel.tsx` | Score thumbnail preview | — | exists |
    72→| `TransitionOverlay.tsx` | Page transition overlay | — | exists |
    73→| `DocumentHeader.tsx` | Document route header | — | exists |
    74→| `DropzoneCopy.tsx` | Dropzone instructional copy | — | exists |
    75→
    76→---
    77→
    78→## Research
    79→
    80→| Resource | Purpose |
    81→|---|---|
    82→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-001 (VexFlow migration), AC-003 (RedLineTooltip), AC-006 (maximize) |
    83→| VexFlow API docs | `Renderer`, `Stave`, `Voice`, `Formatter`, `StaveNote` |
    84→| `.claude/rules/music-theory.md` | SATB voice ranges for VexFlow stave positioning |
    85→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/lib/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/lib/
     2→
     3→*Utilities, adapters, and stubs. Pure TypeScript — no React components, no Zustand stores, no DOM access.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Utility** | A pure function with no side effects — `utils.ts` exports `cn()` for class merging |
    12→| **Adapter** | A typed interface between a third-party API and the HarmonyForge domain — e.g., a Tone.js scheduling adapter |
    13→| **Stub** | A typed placeholder that throws `not implemented` — `llm-review.ts` is a stub until a visual AC requires it |
    14→| **LLM-as-Judge** | The pattern of using a multimodal model to evaluate subjective acceptance criteria (screenshot → pass/fail) |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **`llm-review.ts` is a stub** (ADR-001 consequence): implemented only when the first acceptance criterion cannot be verified programmatically (e.g., "score canvas renders voices with distinct colors and no overlapping noteheads")
    21→- **`utils.ts` exports `cn()`**: uses `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` — the single class merging utility for the entire project
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Patterns
    26→
    27→- All lib functions are pure (no side effects at module load, no global state mutation)
    28→- All lib exports are typed — no `any`, explicit return types on all public functions
    29→- Subdirectory organization: `audio/` for Tone.js adapters, `music/` for data structures, `theory/` for display helpers
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Policies
    34→
    35→- **No React hooks** in `lib/` — hooks live in `src/hooks/` (when created) or co-located with components
    36→- **No Zustand store imports** in `lib/` — lib is framework-agnostic utility code
    37→- **No DOM access** — lib functions run in any environment (Node, browser, test runner)
    38→- **No constraint logic** — `src/lib/theory/` holds display helpers only; detection logic is backend-only
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## Files
    43→
    44→| File | Purpose |
    45→|---|---|
    46→| `utils.ts` | `cn()` class merging utility (`clsx` + `tailwind-merge`) |
    47→| `llm-review.ts` | LLM-as-judge stub — not yet implemented |
    48→| `audio/` | Tone.js symbolic scheduling adapters (empty — Phase 2) |
    49→| `music/` | Music data structures and factory functions (`noteFactory.ts`) |
    50→| `theory/` | Frontend theory display helpers (empty — Phase 3) |

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/lib/audio/
     2→
     3→*Tone.js symbolic scheduling adapters. Currently empty — reserved for Phase 2 `usePlayback` hook output functions.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Symbolic Scheduling** | Dispatching note events as pitch/duration/timing data to Tone.js Transport — no waveform synthesis |
    12→| **Transport** | Tone.js's global playback clock — `Tone.Transport.start()`, `.stop()`, `.position` |
    13→| **Part** | A `Tone.Part` instance — a sequence of `{ time, note, duration }` events for one SATB voice |
    14→| **Playhead** | The current Transport position, mapped back to a measure/beat for the score canvas cursor |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **Symbolic only**: All Tone.js usage is `Tone.Synth` + `Tone.Part` with MIDI note names — no `AudioContext`, no raw buffers, no sample loading
    21→- **Audio adapters live here, not in components**: The `usePlayback` hook (to be created) calls functions from this directory
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Patterns
    26→
    27→- Functions export typed scheduling actions:
    28→  ```ts
    29→  export function scheduleVoice(voice: SATBVoice, notes: NoteEvent[]): Tone.Part
    30→  export function clearSchedule(): void
    31→  ```
    32→- All functions are pure setup/teardown — no global state held inside this directory
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Policies
    37→
    38→- **Never call `new AudioContext()` directly** — use `Tone.start()` which handles the AudioContext lifecycle
    39→- **Never load audio samples** — symbolic synthesis only (`Tone.Synth`, `Tone.PolySynth`)
    40→- **No React imports** — this is a plain TypeScript adapter layer
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→## Research
    45→
    46→| Resource | Purpose |
    47→|---|---|
    48→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-002 (playback hook requirements, symbolic event shape) |
    49→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Tone.js symbolic-only constraint |
    50→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./src/lib/music/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/lib/music/
     2→
     3→*Music domain data structures and factory functions. Pure TypeScript — no framework dependencies, no audio, no rendering.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **NoteEvent** | A symbolic music event: `{ pitch: string, duration: string, voice: SATBVoice, measure: number, beat: number }` |
    12→| **SATBVoice** | One of `"soprano" \| "alto" \| "tenor" \| "bass"` |
    13→| **noteFactory** | Pure factory function(s) in `noteFactory.ts` that construct `NoteEvent` objects |
    14→| **Pitch** | A scientific pitch notation string (e.g., `"C4"`, `"G#3"`) — never a frequency value |
    15→| **Duration** | A rhythmic value string (e.g., `"q"` = quarter, `"h"` = half, `"8"` = eighth) — Tone.js / VexFlow compatible |
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Decisions
    20→
    21→- **Data-only layer**: `src/lib/music/` holds domain data structures and constructors — no VexFlow, no Tone.js, no React
    22→- **Factory pattern**: `noteFactory.ts` produces typed `NoteEvent` objects from raw input — validation happens at this boundary
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Patterns
    27→
    28→- Factory functions validate inputs and return typed domain objects:
    29→  ```ts
    30→  export function createNote(pitch: string, duration: string, voice: SATBVoice): NoteEvent
    31→  ```
    32→- SATB voice ranges enforced at factory boundary (Aldwell & Schachter standard ranges):
    33→  - Soprano: B3–G5, Alto: G3–C5, Tenor: C3–G4, Bass: E2–C4
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Policies
    38→
    39→- **No VexFlow imports** — music data structures are rendering-agnostic
    40→- **No Tone.js imports** — music data structures are playback-agnostic
    41→- **No React or Zustand imports**
    42→- **Constraint violation detection belongs in the backend** — this factory only enforces data shape, not voice-leading rules
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Files
    47→
    48→| File | Purpose |
    49→|---|---|
    50→| `noteFactory.ts` | Factory functions for `NoteEvent` construction |
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→## Research
    55→
    56→| Resource | Purpose |
    57→|---|---|
    58→| `.claude/rules/music-theory.md` | SATB range constraints (Aldwell & Schachter reference) |
    59→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-001 (ScoreState note shape consumed by VexFlow) |
    60→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/store/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/store/
     2→
     3→*Zustand state slices. One store per domain context. All state is typed; all actions are synchronous and co-located with the state they modify.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Store Slice** | A single Zustand `create<T>()` call managing one bounded domain of state |
    12→| **Action** | A function inside a Zustand store that calls `set()` to update state |
    13→| **Selector** | A function passed to a `useStore()` hook call to read a specific piece of state |
    14→| **`useSandboxStore`** | The current store — manages context menu state; needs `isPlaying` and `playheadPosition` added (AC-002) |
    15→| **Context Menu** | The right-click menu on the score canvas: `{ isOpen, x, y }` — currently the only state in `useSandboxStore` |
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Decisions
    20→
    21→- **Zustand over Context API**: Selected for selective subscription performance and TypeScript ergonomics
    22→- **One store per domain**: `useSandboxStore` owns sandbox-specific UI state; a separate `useScoreStore` will own note data when created
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Patterns
    27→
    28→- Store shape: typed interface → `create<Interface>((set) => ({ ...state, ...actions }))`
    29→- Selectors: always use inline selectors to prevent unnecessary re-renders:
    30→  ```ts
    31→  const isOpen = useSandboxStore(s => s.contextMenu.isOpen); // ✓
    32→  const store = useSandboxStore(); // ✗ — subscribes to everything
    33→  ```
    34→- Actions call `set()` directly — no async thunks, no middleware
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Policies
    39→
    40→- **No API calls inside store actions** — stores hold client state only; data fetching belongs in server components or React Query
    41→- **No VexFlow or Tone.js imports** inside stores — stores hold data, not rendering or scheduling logic
    42→- **Typed slices only** — no `any` in store interfaces or action parameters
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Files
    47→
    48→| File | Current State | Needs (AC-002) |
    49→|---|---|---|
    50→| `useSandboxStore.ts` | `contextMenu: { isOpen, x, y }` | Add `isPlaying: boolean`, `playheadPosition: number`, `setIsPlaying`, `setPlayheadPosition` |
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→## Research
    55→
    56→| Resource | Purpose |
    57→|---|---|
    58→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-002 (required store fields: `isPlaying`, `playheadPosition`) |
    59→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Zustand typed slice pattern |
    60→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/adr/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — docs/adr/
     2→
     3→*Architecture Decision Records. Permanent, immutable logs of why technical decisions were made. The audit trail that answers "why" for all time.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **ADR** | Architecture Decision Record — one document per architectural decision; records context, options, rationale, and consequences |
    12→| **Status: Proposed** | Draft — not yet binding; may be changed or withdrawn |
    13→| **Status: Accepted** | In effect — rationale, options, and consequences are frozen; only status line may be edited |
    14→| **Status: Superseded by ADR-NNN** | Replaced — old record preserved as-is; new ADR references it |
    15→| **Status: Deprecated** | No longer relevant — not replaced |
    16→| **Supersede** | The only correct way to "update" a decision: write a new ADR, freeze the old one |
    17→| **Decision Driver** | The forcing function that made the decision necessary |
    18→| **Consequence** | The observable outcome (positive or negative) of adopting the decision |
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Decisions
    23→
    24→- **ADR-001**: Adopt Ralph Wiggum Loop as primary autonomous execution pattern — [Accepted](ADR-001-adopt-ralph-wiggum-loop.md)
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Patterns
    29→
    30→- File naming: `ADR-NNN-kebab-case-title.md` (zero-padded three digits)
    31→- Template: use `template.md` for every new ADR
    32→- After writing a new ADR: update `overview.md` index immediately
    33→- Status line format: `**Status:** Accepted` — bold, on line 4 of the file
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Policies
    38→
    39→- **Never edit rationale, options, or consequences of an Accepted ADR**
    40→- **Never delete an ADR** — change status to Superseded or Deprecated only
    41→- **Supersede, don't patch**: when a decision changes, write ADR-NNN+1 that explicitly states "Supersedes ADR-NNN"
    42→- **One decision per ADR** — compound decisions get separate records
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Files
    47→
    48→| File | Purpose |
    49→|---|---|
    50→| `overview.md` | ADR index — all ADRs with status, date, and owners |
    51→| `template.md` | Canonical Master template for new ADRs |
    52→| `ADR-001-adopt-ralph-wiggum-loop.md` | First ADR — Ralph Wiggum Loop adoption (Accepted) |
    53→
    54→---
    55→
    56→## Research
    57→
    58→| Resource | Purpose |
    59→|---|---|
    60→| `docs/context-engineering-guide.md` | ADR Lifecycle section, Supersede definition |
    61→| `docs/notes/prompting-guide.md` | Stage 6 — how to write an ADR via prompt |
    62→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→# CONTEXT.md — docs/design/

     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **`.pen` file** | A Pencil design tool file — contains the visual design of HarmonyForge screens |
    12→| **`system.md`** | The design system specification — palette, typography, spacing, radius, motion |
    13→| **Nocturne** | The dark theme palette defined in this spec |
    14→| **Sonata** | The light theme palette defined in this spec |
    15→| **Token** | A named value from the design system (color, spacing, radius) — the source of truth for `globals.css` |
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Decisions
    20→
    21→- **Design tokens flow one way**: `docs/design/system.md` → `src/app/globals.css` → Tailwind utility classes. Never reverse this flow.
    22→- **WCAG §1.4.11 fix**: `--sonata-accent-accessible: #b87800` was added to `globals.css` as a compliant alternative to `#ffb300` (1.66:1 → 3.37:1 contrast ratio on Sonata background)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Patterns
    27→
    28→- When a new token is needed: propose it in chat referencing `system.md`, get Superman approval, then add to `globals.css`
    29→- When comparing design vs. implementation: read `HarmonyForge.pen` via Pencil MCP tools (not text editors)
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Policies
    34→
    35→- **Never auto-apply color values from design files** — surface as `[ui-ux-pro-max suggestion]` and wait for approval
    36→- **Never edit `system.md` as a code change** — design system changes require Superman approval and a corresponding `globals.css` update
    37→- **Read `.pen` files only via Pencil MCP tools** — they are encrypted binary files
    38→
    39→---
    40→
    41→## Files
    42→
    43→| File | Purpose |
    44→|---|---|
    45→| `HarmonyForge.pen` | Pencil design file — visual screens (read via Pencil MCP only) |
    46→| `system.md` | Design system specification — authoritative source for all tokens |

    54→| `src/app/globals.css` | The implemented token set |

     1→# CONTEXT.md — docs/archive/
     2→
     3→*Retired documents. Historical reference only — never modify, never delete.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Archived Document** | A retired file preserved for historical context — superseded plans, old specs, pre-ADR decisions |

    13→---
    14→
    15→## Decisions
    16→
    17→- **Archive instead of delete**: Retired documents move here, never to the trash. History is permanent.
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## Patterns
    22→
    23→- When retiring a document: move it here, add a one-line note at the top: `> Archived [date]: superseded by [new file path]`
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Policies
    28→
    29→- **Read-only** — never edit archived documents
    30→- **Never create new active work here** — archives are historical only
    31→
    32→---
    33→
    34→## Files

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./specs/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — specs/
     2→
     3→*JTBD specification documents. The contract between Superman and the loop. Specs define what "done" means — observable user outcomes with testable acceptance criteria.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Spec** | A document that defines what a user should be able to do when a feature is complete — observable outcomes, not implementation steps |
    12→| **JTBD** | Job to Be Done — the framing for each spec: "A composer opens HarmonyForge and can [observable result]" |
    13→| **Acceptance Criterion (AC)** | A single, testable pass/fail condition — one checkbox per AC |
    14→| **`[x]`** | Done and compliant |
    15→| **`[~]`** | Partially implemented — gaps noted inline |
    16→| **`[ ]`** | Not started |
    17→| **`[!]`** | Exists but violates a constraint — must fix before shipping |
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## Decisions
    22→
    23→- **Specs written before code** (Gear 1): No implementation begins without a reviewed spec. The planner reads specs to write `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`.
    24→- **Specs are the loop's contract**: The loop is only accountable to what the ACs define. A vague AC produces vague output.
    25→- **ACs are checkboxes, not prose**: If an AC requires human judgment to evaluate, it belongs in `src/lib/llm-review.ts` as an LLM-as-judge call.
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Patterns
    30→
    31→- Spec file naming: `NNN-kebab-case-feature.md` (zero-padded three digits)
    32→- Status key at top of every spec (the four checkbox types above)
    33→- Each AC section: numbered (`AC-001`, `AC-002`), with existing file references and status markers
    34→- JTBD format: "A [user] opens HarmonyForge and [observable outcome]"
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Policies
    39→
    40→- **No implementation details in specs** — "what", not "how"
    41→- **Never delete an AC** — mark as `[x]` when done; `[!]` when violated
    42→- **ACs must be verifiable without human judgment** (or delegate to `llm-review.ts`)
    43→- **Vague ACs are broken ACs** — "looks good" is not an AC; "VexFlow renders four staves at distinct y-positions" is
    44→
    45→---
    46→
    47→## Files
    48→
    49→| File | Scope | Status |
    50→|---|---|---|
    51→| `001-satb-sandbox.md` | Phase 2–4 — full Tactile Sandbox feature set | Active — Phase 2 in progress |
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→## Research
    56→
    57→| Resource | Purpose |
    58→|---|---|
    59→| `docs/context-engineering-guide.md` | Spec and JTBD definitions, common mistakes |
    60→| `PROMPT_plan.md` | The planner reads this directory to generate `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` |
    61→| `docs/notes/prompting-guide.md` | Stage 5 — how to write a new spec |
    62→
```
</details></tool-use>

    20→
    21→---

    26→- SVG logos imported as `<img>` tags or via Next.js `<Image>` component for optimization

    33→- **No design tokens in SVG files** — SVGs use inline colors or `currentColor`; never hardcoded Nocturne/Sonata values
